articles.title: Temperature Field Reconstruction of Tungsten Monoblock Divertor on EAST using Physics-aware Neural Operator Transformer
articles.excerpt: Accurate modeling of the divertor temperature field is essential for preventing material melting and damage and for extending the service life of fusion devices. However, conventional numerical methods, such as the Finite Element Method (FEM), are computationally expensive and therefore unsuitable for real-time applications. Therefore, a fast and generalizable method is required for real-time reconstruction of the divertor temperature field and subsequent real-time control. To address the above
articles.summary: 提出物理感知神经算子Transformer，用于EAST装置钨偏滤器温度场实时重建，替代计算昂贵的有限元法，实现高精度快速温度场预测，防止材料熔化损伤。

articles.title: Event-Triggered Gain Scheduling of 2 x 2 Linear Hyperbolic PDEs via Neural Operators (Full Version)
articles.excerpt: This paper introduces a new framework for event-triggered gain scheduling applied to linear hyperbolic Partial Differential Equations (PDEs) with time- and space-varying coefficients. The approach leverages neural operators to address the challenges of real-time control in such systems. At each triggering time, the control input is designed using the classical static backstepping control law, while the gains of the boundary controller are updated according to the triggering mechanism and the spa
